name: nextjs-ts-prisma
description: Full-stack Next.js 14 (App Router) scaffold with TypeScript, Prisma ORM (SQLite for dev), Tailwind-free pure server components + Route Handlers, ESLint flat config, Vitest. Production-shape starting point for full-stack apps where the same repo serves UI and API.
included: Prisma ORM with SQLite datasource (swap to Postgres by changing one line)
Example Item model with GET/POST Route Handlers
Server Component page that lists items via the Prisma client
selection_hints: TypeScript backend with the same repo serving the frontend
Prisma or any TypeScript ORM mentioned
Web app needing both UI and an API in one codebase
anti_hints: Python or non-TypeScript language explicitly requested
Heavy CPU workload best suited to Go / Rust

verification.description: Stages: lint (next lint), typecheck (tsc --noEmit), smoke (next build), test (vitest run), all (lint + typecheck + smoke + test). Exit 0 on stage=all means the scaffold builds, types check, and tests pass.

primary_files: README.md — replace project description with what the brief actually builds
prisma/schema.prisma — define your domain models (the Item model is a placeholder; rename or replace)
app/api/items/route.ts — pattern for Route Handlers; rename and add others as needed
app/page.tsx — landing page; replace the items list with your real UI
package.json — update name + description; add runtime deps as needed
tests/items.test.ts — replace with tests for your real routes / components
preserve: lib/prisma.ts — PrismaClient singleton pattern (Next.js dev hot-reload safety)
verify.sh stage routing — multi-stage contract the agentic Coder relies on
